# Cross-product hunting: inventory datasets, then describe the target

XSIAM ingests far more than endpoint telemetry — a tenant commonly exposes hundreds of datasets. `xsiam.get_datasets` enumerates them: `xdr_data` (XDR endpoint), `cloud_audit_logs` (cloud control-plane), `host_firewall_events`, `endpoints`/`host_inventory` (asset state), the `forensics_*` family (DFIR artifacts: `forensics_event_log`, `forensics_dns_cache`, `forensics_amcache`, `forensics_handles`, browser history…), plus per-vendor `*_raw` datasets. Field families are product-specific — the `actor_process_*` / `action_*` / `ENUM.*` conventions are `xdr_data`-only — so for anything outside `xdr_data` the flow is: `get_datasets` (find the source) -> `datamodel_describe(<dataset>)` (learn its fields) -> author. The XQL language (stages/functions) is identical across datasets; only the field names change.

```sql
dataset = cloud_audit_logs
| filter event_type != null
| comp count() as events by event_type, identity_name
| sort desc events
```

_Field-discovery behaviour live-verified on a real XSIAM tenant: `| fields * | limit 1` returned ~1158 native xdr_data columns; `get_datasets` returned 620 datasets; the `| datamodel` stage returned `bad query syntax` (hence the sampling fallback). Adapt the second query's fields to your cloud dataset's real schema (discover them first)._
